qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded archive download causes memory exhaustion (DoS)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded HTTP response reads in guarddog/scanners/scanner.py causing memory exhaustion, while the advisory concerns guarddog/utils/archives.py safe_extract() misusing ZipFile.extract() and enabling zip path traversal file overwrite.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory root cause is a path traversal/arbitrary overwrite in guarddog/utils/archives.py safe_extract() caused by passing os.path.join(target_directory, file) as the ZipFile.extract() path. The only qualifying finding targets a separate scanner.py download path where response.raw.read() reads an attacker-sized archive into memory.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-xg9w-vg3g-6m68 identifies guarddog/utils/archives.py safe_extract() and the ZipFile.extract(file, path=os.path.join(target_directory, file)) misuse as the vulnerable code/root cause.
unbounded_memory_download/vulnerability_report.json identifies guarddog/scanners/scanner.py:203 response.raw.read() without size limits as the vulnerable operation.
The reported impact and sink differ: advisory path traversal writes files outside the intended extraction directory, while the finding demonstrates memory exhaustion during remote archive download.
